quotes.quote: There's footage and records of objects in the skies that we don't know exactly what they are. We can't explain how they moved, their trajectory. They did not have an easily explainable pattern. And so I think that people still take seriously trying to investigate and understand what these are.
quotes.context: Obama became the first sitting or former U.S. president to publicly acknowledge unexplained aerial phenomena in concrete terms.

quotes.role: U.S. Senator (R-FL), Chair, Senate Intelligence Committee
quotes.quote: We have things flying over our military bases and places where we're conducting military exercises and we don't know what it is and it isn't ours. So that to me is a national security risk and one that we should be looking into.
quotes.context: Rubio was instrumental in mandating the DNI UAP report and pushing for government accountability on UAPs.

quotes.role: U.S. Senate Majority Leader (ret.), Sponsor of AATIP
quotes.quote: I'm glad the Pentagon is now releasing this footage, but it only scratches the surface of research and materials available. The U.S. needs to take a serious, scientific look at this and any potential national security implications.
quotes.context: Reid secured the original $22 million in funding for AATIP and spent years advocating for UAP transparency.

quotes.quote: For too long, the American public has been kept in the dark about what our government knows or doesn't know about UAPs. It is time to change that. The American people deserve to know.
quotes.context: Schumer introduced the UAP Disclosure Act of 2023, modeled on the JFK Records Act, requiring mandatory declassification.

quotes.quote: We're not the great superpower that we think we are. These things are able to turn off our nuclear warheads... There's a cover-up. No question about it. It's not a right or left thing. It's a government secrecy thing.
quotes.context: Burchett has been one of the most outspoken members of Congress on UAP transparency and alleged government cover-ups.

quotes.role: U.S. Senator (D-NY), Senate Armed Services Committee
quotes.quote: We have legislation which creates this oversight board that would declassify these materials. We want to make sure that the American public has access to this information and that we're taking it seriously. This is a national security issue.
quotes.context: Gillibrand co-authored UAP-related NDAA amendments and has consistently pushed for accountability and public disclosure.

quotes.quote: I saw an unidentified flying object. It was the darndest thing I've ever seen. It was big, it was very bright, it changed colors and it was about the size of the moon. We watched it for ten minutes, but none of us could figure out what it was.
quotes.context: Carter filed an official UFO sighting report in 1969 and promised to release all UFO files if elected. As president, he was reportedly denied access to certain programs.

quotes.role: Former NRO & NGA Officer, UAP Task Force Member
quotes.quote: I was informed, in the course of my official duties, by multiple current and former senior officials, that our government has indeed been operating with the knowledge of non-human intelligence for decades. I was also informed of a multi-decade, UAP crash retrieval and reverse-engineering program.
quotes.source: U.S. House Committee on Oversight Testimony
quotes.context: Grusch filed an official whistleblower complaint and testified under oath before Congress, making him the first U.S. intelligence official to do so on record about non-human intelligence.

quotes.role: Former Director, AATIP (Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program)
quotes.quote: These objects — whatever they are — are displaying capabilities that are not just beyond our current capabilities, but possibly beyond our current understanding of physics. The question isn't whether they're real. That debate is over. The question is what are they, where do they come from, and what do they want?
quotes.context: Elizondo resigned from AATIP in 2017 in protest over excessive secrecy and joined Tom DeLonge's TTSA to push for public disclosure.

quotes.role: Former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense for Intelligence
quotes.quote: The technology we observed is not something the United States has — not even close. And it's not something that Russia or China has. We're not dealing with a known adversary. These craft are defying the laws of physics as we understand them. That should terrify and intrigue every scientist on the planet.
quotes.context: Mellon was instrumental in getting UAP footage to the New York Times in 2017 and has been a leading voice for congressional accountability.

quotes.role: Former U.S. Navy F/A-18 Pilot, Americans for Safe Aerospace
quotes.quote: I can tell you that whatever I saw and my squadron saw was not from this world. I have never said that before publicly. These objects showed up in our restricted airspace day after day. They were clearly structured craft — not natural phenomena, not illusions. We were being outflown by something we couldn't identify.
quotes.context: Graves led a squadron that encountered UAPs daily off the East Coast from 2014-2015. He founded Americans for Safe Aerospace to advocate for UAP reporting.

quotes.role: Podcast Host, The Joe Rogan Experience
quotes.quote: Bob Lazar said there were spacecraft of non-human origin being reverse-engineered at a facility called S-4. Nobody believed him. That was 1989. Now the U.S. government is confirming unknown craft in restricted airspace and senators are introducing UAP Disclosure Acts. Maybe it's time to revisit everything Bob said.
quotes.context: Rogan's three-hour interview with Bob Lazar and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell reached tens of millions of listeners and reignited mainstream interest in UAP disclosure.

quotes.role: Political Commentator, Former Fox News Host
quotes.quote: Here's the first question: what are they? No one in the government will say. They're not from here — our government officials have admitted that in effect. And yet somehow this isn't considered the single most important news story of our time. Why not? That's the question that bothers me most.
quotes.context: Carlson ran extensive UAP coverage on Fox News and interviewed multiple whistleblowers, reaching a large conservative audience previously skeptical of UAP claims.

quotes.quote: I have spoken to enough credible, senior military and intelligence officials — people with the highest clearances, people who administered these programs — to be convinced that there is something profound going on. The U.S. government has information about non-human intelligence that it has not shared with the public, Congress, or even most of the executive branch.
quotes.context: Coulthart is an award-winning journalist who broke major UAP stories and authored 'In Plain Sight' — one of the most comprehensive books on UAP disclosure efforts.

quotes.quote: The moment that changed everything for me wasn't the Tic-Tac. It was when I learned that military personnel who reported UAP encounters were being threatened and told to stay quiet. This isn't fringe conspiracy stuff. This is documented intimidation of federal witnesses. That's when I knew the cover-up was real.
quotes.context: Corbell leaked several significant UAP videos including the 'Pyramid' UAP video from the USS Russell, later confirmed authentic by the Pentagon.
